items.title: Etsy Migrates 1000-Shard, 425 TB MySQL Sharding Architecture to Vitess
items.summaryShort: Etsy Migrates 1000-Shard, 425 TB MySQL Sharding Architecture to Vitess. The Etsy engineering team recently described how the company migrated its long-running MySQL sharding infrastructure to Vitess.

items.title: Presentation: Latency: The Race to Zero...Are We There Yet?
items.summaryShort: Presentation: Latency: The Race to Zero...Are We There Yet?. Amir Langer discusses the evolution of latency reduction, from the Pony Express to modern hardware.

items.title: CNCF and Kusari Partner to Strengthen Software Supply Chain Security Across Cloud-Native Projects
items.summaryShort: CNCF and Kusari Partner to Strengthen Software Supply Chain Security Across Cloud-Native Projects. The Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) and Kusari have announced a new collaboration aimed at strengthening software supply chain secur

items.title: Google Cloud Highlights Ongoing Work on PostgreSQL Core Capabilities
items.summaryShort: Google Cloud Highlights Ongoing Work on PostgreSQL Core Capabilities. Google Cloud has outlined its recent technical contributions to PostgreSQL, emphasizing improvements in logical replication, upgrade processes, and overall system stabili

items.title: Safari Adds scrollend Event Support, Completing Baseline Browser Coverage
items.summaryShort: Safari Adds scrollend Event Support, Completing Baseline Browser Coverage. Safari's release of version 26.2 in December introduced support for the scrollend event, completing its alignment with major browsers.

items.title: NASA Artemis II splashes down in Pacific Ocean in ‘perfect' landing for Moon mission
items.summaryShort: NASA Artemis II splashes down in Pacific Ocean in ‘perfect' landing for Moon mission. The Integrity craft splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego just after 5:07 p.m.

items.title: How to watch NASA's Artemis II splash back down to Earth
items.summaryShort: How to watch NASA's Artemis II splash back down to Earth. NASA's Artemis II mission has traveled farther from Earth than any mission before.

items.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude
items.summaryShort: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude. This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.
